brief.audience: Content teams, editors, and marketers who manage editorial workflows
brief.businessGoal: Provide a powerful, polished multi-view database for content/editorial workflows that rivals and surpasses Airtable in usability and visual quality

output.previewStrategy: Generate a Canvas HTML workspace file after generation tools are enabled.

skill.title: kanban-board
skill.rationale: Best fit for multi-view database app with kanban board as one of the three core views

styleReference.rationale: User explicitly selected Airtable design system; Stripe and Supabase provide supplementary polish cues for toolbars, color, and spacing

requiredReads.reason: Primary style reference — Airtable design system.

artifacts.purpose: Complete single-file Airtable-like database application for content/editorial workflow management, featuring Grid, Kanban, and Calendar views with interactive data editing
requirements: Sidebar navigation with bases/tables list, matching Airtable's left sidebar pattern
Grid view: full editable spreadsheet with column headers, field types (text, select/status, date, collaborator, attachment thumbnail, checkbox), row numbering, and column resize handles
Kanban view: cards grouped by status columns (Idea, Drafting, In Review, Published, Archived) with drag-ready card UI, cover thumbnails, tags, and metadata
Calendar view: monthly calendar layout with content pieces placed on their publish dates, color-coded by status or content type
View switcher toolbar with Grid/Kanban/Calendar toggle tabs, plus filter/sort/group controls
Sample editorial dataset: ~15-20 rows with realistic content titles, authors, statuses, content types (Blog, Newsletter, Social, Video, Podcast), publish dates, word counts, and assignees
Expandable record detail panel/modal when clicking a row or card
Top bar with base name, breadcrumbs, share button, and user avatars
Field type icons and color-coded status pills consistent with Airtable's visual language
Responsive layout that works on desktop and tablet widths
All interactions handled via vanilla JS — view switching, inline editing, record expansion, sidebar collapse

screens: Main app shell with sidebar, toolbar, and view area
Calendar view — monthly calendar with event cards
Record detail expand — side panel or modal for full record view
sections: Left sidebar: workspace/base switcher, table list, view list
Top toolbar: view tabs (Grid/Kanban/Calendar), search, filter, sort, group, share
Main content: switches between grid, kanban, and calendar based on active view tab
Record detail: slide-in panel from right with all fields editable

interaction.primaryFlow: User navigates to the content base, switches between Grid/Kanban/Calendar views, edits records inline or via the detail panel, and filters/groups content by status, type, or assignee
states: Default: Grid view loaded with sample editorial data
View switch: animated tab transition between Grid, Kanban, Calendar
Inline edit: click cell to edit, blur to save
Record expanded: side panel slides in with full record details
Filtered/sorted: toolbar controls adjust visible rows or card groupings
Sidebar collapsed: icon-only sidebar for more workspace room

acceptanceCriteria: All three views (Grid, Kanban, Calendar) render correctly with the same underlying dataset
View switching is instant with no page reload
Grid cells are editable with appropriate input types per field
Status pills use distinct, accessible colors matching Airtable conventions
Calendar shows content items on correct dates with status color coding
Kanban cards show key metadata (title, type tag, assignee avatar, due date)
Record detail panel shows all fields and allows editing
No text overlap, clipping, or broken layout at 1280px and above
Design quality check has P0=0 and all P1 issues are fixed or explicitly waived

risks: Single HTML file may be large — mitigate with efficient CSS and minimal inline assets
Calendar view complexity — use a clean CSS grid approach rather than a heavy library
Inline editing across field types needs careful JS handling
abstract: This plan creates a high-fidelity, Airtable-inspired database application tailored for content and editorial teams. The single-file HTML app will feature three fully interactive views — Grid (editable spreadsheet), Kanban (status-grouped card board), and Calendar (monthly publish schedule) — all driven by a realistic sample dataset of editorial content. The design follows the Airtable design system for visual language, with sidebar navigation, a view-switching toolbar, and expandable record detail panels. Before approving, confirm that the three-view scope, editorial dataset, and Airtable visual direction match your vision.
